pattern: Each value floods outward from every one of its own occurrences simultaneously; the slowest gap between two neighboring copies of the best value sets the clock.
hints: Think of every occurrence of a value as a source that spreads one step per second in both directions around the circle. A value wins once its sources have covered the whole array.
For a fixed value, only the largest circular gap between two consecutive occurrences of that value matters - that gap is the bottleneck.

Group indices by value. For each value, look at every consecutive pair of its occurrences around the circle (wrapping past the end back to the start); the seconds needed for that value is floor(largest gap / 2). Answer = smallest such cost over all values.

edge_inputs.description: one value dominates but leaves an uneven gap
two distinct values each appearing once
the globally most frequent value is not the fastest to spread - a rarer, evenly spaced value wins
single occurrence of the majority value forces using the full circle as the gap
odd-length circular gap needs floor division, not ceiling

stress.description: 100k elements, few distinct values placed at random circular positions
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, size)
    values = [rng.randrange(1, 6) for _ in range(3)]
    nums = [values[rng.randrange(0, len(values))] for _ in range(n)]
    return [nums]
stress.note: few distinct values maximize the per-value gap bookkeeping; should finish well under the run timeout

stress.description: 100k elements almost all distinct, forcing large single-occurrence gaps
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, size)
    nums = list(range(1, n + 1))
    rng.shuffle(nums)
    i = rng.randrange(0, n)
    j = rng.randrange(0, n)
    nums[j] = nums[i]
    return [nums]
stress.note: mostly-unique values with one duplicated pair exercises the many-singleton-value path
